title: T-031 — Add multi_site permission strings to enum (§10A.5, §10B RBAC tables, §11.5)
prompt:
# T-031 — Add multi_site permission strings to enum

## Goal
Append a delimited `MULTI-SITE` permission group to `packages/rbac/src/permissions.enum.ts` covering the 26 canonical RBAC strings declared by `docs/prd/14-MULTI-SITE-PRD.md` (§10A.5 lane/rate-card RBAC, §10B MS-101..110 admin/operational surfaces, §11.5 activation, §14.2 super-admin audit). Settings/Auth owns the enum file; this task only contributes a new namespaced section under `multi_site.*` prefix. Export `ALL_MULTI_SITE_PERMISSIONS` so the canonical permissions.enum owner (02-settings T-001) and the ESLint enum-lock guard (02-settings T-130) recognize the module group. Closes audit `_meta/audits/2026-05-14-prd-vs-tasks-coverage-gaps.md` MS-008 slice.

## Implementation contract
- File: `packages/rbac/src/permissions.enum.ts` — append entries to `Permission` const object with JSDoc citing PRD anchor. Do NOT remove or rename any existing string.
- File: `packages/rbac/src/__tests__/permissions.test.ts` — extend the suite to assert presence, regex `^[a-z_]+\.[a-z_]+\.[a-z_]+$`, uniqueness, and `ALL_MULTI_SITE_PERMISSIONS` typed `readonly Permission[]` with length === 26.
- File: `CODEOWNERS` — verify `/packages/rbac/src/permissions.enum.ts @monopilot/architect` line exists.

## Permission strings (26)
- `multi_site.site.view` — §10B MS-101..104, §10C MS-114
- `multi_site.site.create` — §10B MS-101, §13.5 activation
- `multi_site.site.edit` — §10C MS-114, §10C MS-115
- `multi_site.site.decommission` — §10B MS-104, V-MS-21
- `multi_site.site_access.assign` — §10B MS-101
- `multi_site.site_access.revoke` — §10B MS-101
- `multi_site.site_access.bulk_assign` — §10B MS-101
- `multi_site.site_settings.override` — §10B MS-102
- `multi_site.site_settings.clear` — §10B MS-102
- `multi_site.ist.create` — §10A.5 / 05-WH cross-ref
- `multi_site.ist.amend` — §10B MS-107, V-MS-23
- `multi_site.ist.cancel` — §10B MS-107
- `multi_site.ist.approve` — §11.3 V-MS-10/11
- `multi_site.lane.create` — §10A.5
- `multi_site.lane.edit` — §10A.5
- `multi_site.lane.deactivate` — §10A.5, V-MS-29
- `multi_site.rate_card.upload` — §10A.5
- `multi_site.rate_card.approve` — §10A.3.3 / §10A.5
- `multi_site.rate_card.delete` — §10A.5
- `multi_site.replication.retry` — §10B MS-106, V-MS-22
- `multi_site.replication.run_sync` — §10B MS-106
- `multi_site.conflict.resolve` — §10B MS-103, V-MS-30
- `multi_site.activation.start` — §13.5 D-MS-14, V-MS-18
- `multi_site.activation.rollback` — §10B MS-108, V-MS-20
- `multi_site.config.promote` — §10B MS-105, D-MS-17
- `multi_site.cross_site.read` — §14.2 super-admin audit

## Files in scope
- `packages/rbac/src/permissions.enum.ts` [modify]
- `packages/rbac/src/__tests__/permissions.test.ts` [modify]
- `CODEOWNERS` [modify if missing entry]

## Acceptance criteria
<!-- AC count consolidated from 4→4 by Fixer F14a 2026-05-14 (no coverage lost) --> (Given-When-Then)
- AC1 — Given the enum file is imported, when the consumer reads the new keys, then all 26 strings listed above are present exactly once.
- AC2 — Given each new permission string is matched against regex `^[a-z_]+\.[a-z_]+\.[a-z_]+$`, when tested, then every string passes and zero duplicates exist across `Object.values(Permission)`.
- AC3 — Given the module export, when imported, then `ALL_MULTI_SITE_PERMISSIONS` is exported typed `readonly Permission[]` with length === 26.
- AC4 — Given `CODEOWNERS` is read, when checked, then `packages/rbac/src/permissions.enum.ts` is architect-only (`@monopilot/architect`).

## Test strategy (RED first, vitest)
1. Extend `packages/rbac/src/__tests__/permissions.test.ts` with the four ACs. Run RED:
   `pnpm --filter @monopilot/web vitest run packages/rbac/src/__tests__/permissions.test.ts`
   — must fail before any enum change.
2. Implement minimum changes in `permissions.enum.ts`.
3. Re-run vitest — must pass.
4. Run `pnpm typecheck` and `pnpm lint` in the changed package; both must pass.

## Risk red lines
- Do not weaken the regex `^[a-z_]+\.[a-z_]+\.[a-z_]+$` or introduce a 4-segment / matrix permission model.
- Do not introduce a parallel permission model (no per-module `*PermissionEnum` in another file).
- Do not remove or rename existing strings (Settings/Foundation/01-NPD/03-Technical et al. strings stay intact).
- Do not edit permission strings owned by other modules.
- Do not commit secrets or vault keys.

## Closeout evidence required
- changed_files (with line counts)
- exact RED→GREEN test commands and outputs
- acceptance_criteria_status (one row per AC)
- deviations_from_prd (or "none")
- git_status

pipeline_inputs.description: Append 26 multi_site.*.* permission strings to packages/rbac/src/permissions.enum.ts derived from §10A.5 RBAC table + §10B MS-101..110 RBAC + §11.5/§14.2 activation/audit + export ALL_MULTI_SITE_PERMISSIONS for ESLint enum-lock guard. Closes audit slice MS-008.
pipeline_inputs.details: Append 26 multi_site.*.* permission strings to packages/rbac/src/permissions.enum.ts derived from §10A.5 RBAC table + §10B MS-101..110 RBAC + §11.5/§14.2 activation/audit + export ALL_MULTI_SITE_PERMISSIONS for ESLint enum-lock guard. Closes audit slice MS-008. Scope: Append a delimited `MULTI-SITE` permission group to `packages/rbac/src/permissions. Touches: packages/rbac/src/permissions.enum.ts, packages/rbac/src/__tests__/permissions.test.ts, CODEOWNERS. Constraints: - File: `packages/rbac/src/permissions.enum.ts` — append entries to `Permission` const object with JSDoc citing PRD anchor. Do NOT remove or rename any existing string. - File: `packages/rbac/src/__tests__/permissions.test.ts` — extend the suite to assert presence, regex `^[a-z_]+\.[a-z_]+\.[a-z_]+$`, uniqueness, and `ALL_MULTI_SITE_PERMISSIONS` ty. Distinguished from sibling 14-multi-site tasks by its narrow permissions-enum focus against PRD §10A.5, §10B MS-101, §10B MS-102, §10B MS-103. AC count consolidated from 4→4 by Fixer F14a 2026-05-14 (no coverage lost)
out_of_scope: Do not introduce a 4-level matrix permission model.
Do not edit other modules' permission strings.
Do not move permissions into Reference.RolePermissions table (enum is authoritative).
Do not couple enum strings to specific UI components.

cross_module_dependencies.reason: Settings owns the enum file; this task appends a delimited namespaced group following the T-001 pattern.

ESLint enum-lock guard (`no-direct-permissions-enum-edit`) registered in packages/rbac/.eslintrc.cjs recognises module groups via ALL_<MODULE>_PERMISSIONS exports — this task adds ALL_MULTI_SITE_PERMISSIONS.

acceptance_criteria: All 26 permission strings listed in pipeline_inputs.permission_strings are present in packages/rbac/src/permissions.enum.ts, with JSDoc citing the PRD anchor.
Every new string matches regex ^[a-z_]+\\.[a-z_]+\\.[a-z_]+$ and no duplicates exist across Object.values(Permission); vitest assertion enforces both.
ALL_MULTI_SITE_PERMISSIONS is exported from packages/rbac/src/permissions.enum.ts as readonly Permission[] with length === 26; type-check passes.
test_strategy: RED: extend packages/rbac/src/__tests__/permissions.test.ts with presence + regex + uniqueness + array-length assertions for the 26 new strings. Run `pnpm --filter @monopilot/web vitest run packages/rbac/src/__tests__/permissions.test.ts`; expect failure.
GREEN: append the strings + ALL_MULTI_SITE_PERMISSIONS export; re-run vitest; expect pass.
Run `pnpm typecheck` and `pnpm lint` after the change; both must pass.
risk_red_lines: Do not introduce a parallel permission model (no per-module *PermissionEnum file).
Do not remove or rename existing strings.
Do not edit permission strings owned by other modules.
Do not couple enum strings to specific UI components or table rows.
Do not merge before 02-settings T-130 (ESLint enum-lock guard) is GREEN — the guard is the mechanised governance gate; CODEOWNERS architect-lock on permissions.enum.ts remains the human gate.
